label: 114_billion percent_outlays_rose billion_monthly budget
description: The Congressional Budget Office reviewed federal spending increases in various categories, including unemployment insurance, corporate taxes, and excise taxes. These changes reflected economic conditions and policy decisions over the years.

earliest_articles.title: Monthly Budget Review
earliest_articles.excerpt: that increased federal payments to states beginning in February 2009. Payments for Social Security benefits increased by $23 billion (or 7 percent), and outlays for Medicare rose by $12 billion (or 6 percent). Outlays for net interest on the public debt were $26 billion (or 31 percent) higher than in the first six…
